source_jobs.title: Founding Designer
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are

Over the next few years, every financial institution will have teams of AI analysts working alongside their sharpest minds. At Endex, we're on a mission to bridge the present to the inevitable by building the most sophisticated digital coworker native in Excel. We're a team working on the frontier of agentic development and applied finance, in close collaboration with leading research labs and backed by OpenAI ($14M Raise in Aug. 2025).

With significant demand after our launch, financial firms across the country recognize agentic AI in financial modeling is the future.



About the Role

Location: New York City
Commitment: 5-6 days in-person

As Founding Designer at Endex, you'll define how financial professionals interact with AI in their most critical workflows. You'll design intuitive interfaces for agentic AI, building a design system that scales, and establishing the visual identity of a category-defining product. If you want to shape how Wall Street works with AI, this is your opportunity.



What You'll Own

 - Core Product Experience: Design the end-to-end user journey for interacting with AI agents in Excel. Create intuitive interfaces for complex workflows (prompting, iterating, reviewing AI outputs)

 - Onboarding & Activation: Craft the first-time user experience that takes someone from "what is this?" to "I can't work without this."

 - Design System: Build and maintain our design system from scratch.

 - Visual Identity: Shape Endex's brand through our product, marketing site, and collateral. Create a visual language that feels sophisticated, trustworthy, and cutting-edge

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with Engineering and Product to ship designs. Balance user needs with technical constraints, especially within Excel's add-in environment

 - Growth & Conversion: Design signup flows, pricing pages, and upgrade prompts that drive conversion



Who You Are

 - Product Designer: You have a strong portfolio demonstrating end-to-end product design, from research and wireframes to high-fidelity mockups and shipped features

 - User-Obsessed: You deeply understand user workflows and pain points. You conduct research proactively and let insights drive your design decisions

 - Technically Fluent: You understand technical constraints and can work effectively with engineers. You know what's possible in web technologies and can design within platform limitations

 - Detail-Oriented: Your designs are polished and production-ready

 - Fast Executor: You ship quickly, and iterate based on feedback

 - Strong Communicator: You articulate design decisions clearly and can advocate for user needs across the organization



Requirements

 - 3+ years of product design experience at technology companies, ideally B2B SaaS or technical tools

 - Strong portfolio showcasing end-to-end product design work with measurable impact

 - Proficiency in modern design tools (Figma required)

 - Experience designing complex workflows or enterprise software



Bonus Points

 - Background in finance or financial services—you understand Excel power users and financial modeling workflows

 - Experience designing AI/ML products or agentic interfaces

 - Experience with design systems or component libraries

 - Front-end development skills (HTML/CSS/JavaScript)

 - Experience at early-stage startups (seed to Series A)



Benefits

 - Competitive salary + meaningful equity

 - Health & dental insurance

 - Monthly wellness stipend

 - 401(k) matching

source_jobs.title: Finance Product Specialist (Full/Part-Time)
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are?

Over the next few years, every financial institution will have teams of AI analysts working alongside their sharpest minds. At Endex, we're on a mission to bridge the present to the inevitable by building the most sophisticated digital coworker native in Excel. We're a team working on the frontier of agentic development and applied finance, in close collaboration with leading research labs and backed by OpenAI (Lead Investor in $14M Round).



About the Role

Location: New York City

Commitment: Part-Time (hybrid) or Full-Time (in office)

As a product specialist at Endex, you will play a critical role in ensuring Endex is the most comprehensive, and accurate financial tool across all financial sectors. Your scope will include ensuring Endex supports industry workflows intuitively and completely and validating Endex’s outputs to match the expectations of our strong enterprise pipeline. This role is available on a part-time, asynchronous basis or full-time in-person. In addition, this role can open opportunities to move full-time under various roles as Endex scales.



Your Key Responsibilities

 - Validate Output Quality: Rigorously test and evaluate the model’s financial outputs for correctness, edge cases, and domain consistency.

 - Investigate Errors: Diagnose model failure modes, root-cause them, and propose mitigation strategies.

 - Translate Finance Intuition into Product: Convert your real-world financial experience into heuristics, rules, or guardrails for model behavior.

 - Influence Roadmap Priorities: Recommend where to invest development effort (e.g. sector specialization, comparables, forecasting logic) based on user needs.

 - Collaborate Cross-Functionally: Work closely with ML engineers, data scientists, and product teams to align metrics, trade-offs, and UX.



You Might Be A Fit If…

 - You’ve held roles in investment banking, private equity, sell-side research, public markets investing (mid-cap or larger), or FP&A and strategic or corporate finance.

 - You have deep conviction about what “high-quality financial analysis” looks like and can spot when something is off.

 - You enjoy bridging domain knowledge and technical product design.

 - You like working in early-stage, high-ambiguous settings and want to shape how AI is used in finance.



Full-Time Specific-Benefits

 - Health Care - We offer comprehensive health insurance

 - Monthly Wellness Stipend

 - Retirement - 401K Matching

 - Competitive Salary + Equity



Join us in building the AI-native vertical finance tool, transforming how thousands of firms operate and compete.

source_jobs.title: Member of Technical Staff
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are

Over the next few years, every financial institution will have teams of AI analysts working alongside their sharpest minds. Agents have already reshaped software engineering. The same shift is coming for financial services.

At Endex, we're building the most sophisticated AI coworker for finance. We're a team working at the frontier of agentic development and applied finance, in close collaboration with leading research labs and backed by OpenAI ($14M raised in Aug. 2025).

With significant demand after our launch, the world’s largest financial firms recognize agentic financial modeling is the future.

About the Role

Location: New York City Commitment: 5 Days In-Person

We're looking for engineers who want to work on category-defining AI work. Building agents with the performance and reliability Wall Street demands is one of the hardest applied AI problems out there.

Our technical staff works across the stack: agent architecture, evals, model post-training, and new product surfaces. You'll ship to the largest financial firms in the world.

Example Projects Include…

 - Design and ship the core agentic systems that power Endex across planning, tool use, memory, long-horizon task execution.

 - Partner with customers to understand finance workflows, and leverage learnings to improve our product

 - Build the evals, datasets, and post-training pipelines that turn raw model capability into a product analysts trust

 - As one of our earliest engineers, you'll shape our architecture, our hiring bar, and the engineering culture

You may be a fit if

 - You have architected and built scalable systems

 - You have worked on agentic products or infrastructure and have informed opinions on reliability and performance of complex multi-step systems

 - You have deep experience with API design, service communication patterns, and database optimization

 - You have an iterative mindset where you develop proof of concepts, make decisions quickly, and ship v0s

 - You have strong software engineering fundamentals and enjoy owning the full lifecycle of production services

Full-Time Benefits

 - Health & Dental Insurance

 - Monthly Wellness Stipend

 - 401K Matching

 - Competitive Salary + Equity

source_jobs.title: GTM Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are

Over the next few years, every financial institution will have teams of AI analysts working alongside their sharpest minds. At Endex, we're on a mission to bridge the present to the inevitable by building the most sophisticated digital coworker. We're a team working on the frontier of agentic development and applied finance, in close collaboration with leading research labs and backed by OpenAI ($14M Raise in Aug. 2025).

With significant demand after our launch, financial firms across the country recognize agentic AI in financial modeling is the future.

About the Role

Location: New York City Commitment: 5-6 Days In-Person

GTM Engineers at Endex own the systems that turn pipeline into revenue. This is a hybrid builder role where you'll design, implement, and optimize the automated infrastructure behind our outbound, inbound, and partner motions. You'll work directly with the CEO and GTM team to architect repeatable revenue systems targeting private equity, investment banking, hedge funds, and corporate finance buyers.

Your Key Responsibilities

 - Build & Own Revenue Infrastructure: Design and maintain outbound and inbound pipeline systems using Clay, HubSpot, and automation tooling

 - Optimize the Funnel: Analyze conversion data across the pipeline to identify drop-offs, test hypotheses, and implement fixes that measurably improve reply rates, qualification, and deal velocity.

 - Automate Outbound Campaigns: Build targeted, data-enriched outbound motions aimed at senior finance professionals. Research accounts, enrich contacts, write personalized messaging frameworks, and iterate based on performance.

 - Strengthen Inbound Processing: Develop systems to qualify, prioritize, and route inbound leads so the sales team spends time on the highest-value conversations.

 - Integrate Data Sources: Connect product usage signals, CRM data, and third-party enrichment tools to give the GTM team a unified, actionable view of prospects and customers.

 - Experiment and Iterate: Run structured tests on targeting, messaging, channels, and timing. Document results, build feedback loops, and scale what works.

 - Support GTM Strategy: Collaborate with the CEO and broader team on market expansion, vertical prioritization, and channel partner enablement with data-driven insights.

What We're Looking For

 - Builder Mentality: You've stood up outbound or inbound systems from scratch using tools like Clay, HubSpot, Zapier, n8n, or similar platforms.

 - Commercial Instinct: Every workflow you build ties back to a revenue outcome.

 - Technical Fluency: You're comfortable with APIs, data enrichment, light scripting (Python, SQL, or similar), and connecting systems together

 - Analytical Rigor: You dig into funnel data, spot patterns others miss, and translate findings into concrete next steps.

 - Curiosity & Adaptability: You teach yourself new tools fast and stay on top of what's emerging in the GTM tooling ecosystem.

 - Comfort with Ambiguity: You can take an undefined problem, break it into components, and figure out how to tackle it.

 - Strong Communication: You can explain a complex workflow to an SDR and present funnel analysis to the CEO with equal clarity.

Nice to Haves

 - Background in sales ops, rev ops, marketing ops, or a growth-oriented GTM role

 - Familiarity with financial services buyers or enterprise sales cycles

 - Early-stage startup experience where you built GTM infrastructure

Full-Time Benefits

 - Health & Dental Insurance

 - Monthly Wellness Stipend

 - Retirement Benefits — 401K Matching

 - Competitive Salary + Equity

source_jobs.title: Operations
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are?

Over the next few years, every financial institution will have teams of AI analysts working alongside their sharpest minds. At Endex, we're on a mission to bridge the present to the inevitable by building the most sophisticated digital coworker native in Excel. We're a team working on the frontier of agentic development and applied finance, in close collaboration with leading research labs and backed by OpenAI ($14M Raise in Aug. 2025).

With significant demand after our launch, financial firms across the country recognize agentic AI in financial modeling is the future.



About the Role

Location: New York City

Commitment: 5-6 Days In-Person

Operators at Endex manage strategic initiatives, drive operational excellence, and own business growth. The role flexes across numerous high-impact, high-visibility workstreams, from leading GTM calls, hiring our future team, owning feature roadmaps, establishing new verticals and more. If you thrive in ambiguity and seek the opportunity to build the backbone of a transformational company, let’s chat.



Your Key Responsibilities

 - Execute Strategy: Work directly with the CEO to identify, prioritize, and drive forward high-impact initiative

 - Own Critical Operations: Ensure day-to-day needs across customers, partners, and internal systems are met quickly and effectively.

 - Shape the Product Roadmap: Gather insights from customers, evaluate workflows, and analyze the competitive landscape to inform product direction and strengthen Endex’s position as the premier AI Excel tool.

 - Support Go-To-Market: Join key sales conversations, build and optimize our pipeline systems, and create marketing materials and strategy to accelerate growth.

 - Explore New Verticals: Research and validate new markets and applications for Endex’s frontier agentic services, helping define where we expand next.

 - Recruit and Build the Team: Lead recruiting efforts, identify bottlenecks, and manage hiring processes to attract and select top talent.



What We’re Looking For

 - Athlete: You have a proven track record of excellence in fast-paced environments. You thrive under pressure, set a high bar for yourself, and are driven to help your team succeed.

 - Versatile Problem-Solver: You’re intellectually curious, quick to learn, and eager to take on new challenges across a wide range of responsibilities

 - Relentlessly Resourceful: Ambiguity doesn’t slow you down. You move fast, leverage your network, and figure things out even without a playbook.

 - Execution-Oriented: You translate company strategy into action by identifying, prioritizing, and driving forward key initiatives.

 - Organizer: You help set the company’s operating rhythm, designing and implementing systems

 - Strong communication: Collaboration across functions and teammates is core to Endex

 - Low ego: You listen actively, welcome feedback, and stay focused on outcomes rather than credit. You’re confident in your abilities but humble enough to adapt and learn.



Nice to haves…

 - Early-stage start-up experience

 - Finance experience relevant to Endex’s business



Full-Time Benefits

 - Health & Dental Insurance

 - Monthly Wellness Stipend

 - Retirement Benefits - 401K Matching

 - Competitive Salary + Equity

source_jobs.title: GTM
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are

Over the next few years, every financial institution will have teams of AI analysts working alongside their sharpest minds. At Endex, we're building the most sophisticated digital coworker native in Excel—and we're on a mission to bridge the present to the inevitable. We're a team working at the frontier of agentic development and applied finance, in close collaboration with leading research labs and backed by OpenAI ($14M raise in Aug. 2025).

With significant demand after our launch, financial firms across the country recognize agentic AI in financial modeling is the future.

About the Role

Location: New York City
Commitment: 5-6 Days In-Person

We're looking for someone who's lived manual Excel work. You know the pain points because you've felt them. Now you want revolutionize traditional finance work with frontier AI.

As a GTM hire at Endex, you'll own the full deal lifecycle: sourcing, running demos, negotiating contracts, and ensuring customers actually get value from the product. Your industry experience is critical. You'll work directly with our product and leadership teams to shape what we build, how we position it, and where we take the company next.

Your Key Responsibilities

 - Own Deals End-to-End: Run the full sales cycle from prospecting through close. Build relationships with finance teams, understand their workflows, and show them what's possible with Endex.

 - Shape Product Direction: Your background means you know what financial professionals actually need. Translate that into product feedback, feature requests, and roadmap input that keeps us ahead.

 - Inform GTM Strategy: Help us figure out which segments to prioritize, how to position against alternatives, and what messaging resonates. You're not just executing a playbook—you're helping write it.

 - Build Pipeline Systems: Create and optimize the processes that turn interest into revenue. Partner with marketing to develop content and campaigns that speak to our audience.

 - Expand Into New Verticals: Leverage your network and industry knowledge to identify and validate new markets for Endex.

What We're Looking For

 - 1-6 years in Real Estate, Private Equity, Investment Banking, or Public Equities. You've built models, worked on deals, and understand the day-to-day of financial professionals. This is non-negotiable.

 - Hungry to sell. You're excited to close deals with Wall Street's largest firms. You see GTM as a craft worth mastering.

 - Strong opinions on product. You have ideas about what Endex should build and how we should position it. You're not shy about sharing them.

 - Relentlessly resourceful. You move fast, leverage your network, and figure things out without a playbook.

 - Versatile Problem-Solver: You’re intellectually curious, quick to learn, and eager to take on new challenges across a wide range of responsibilities

 - Low ego, high standards. You listen, adapt, and stay focused on outcomes over credit.

Nice to Have

 - Early-stage startup experience

 - Existing relationships at target firms

Full-Time Benefits

 - Health & Dental Insurance

 - Monthly Wellness Stipend

 - 401K Matching

 - Competitive Salary + Equity

source_jobs.title: Marketing
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are?

Over the next few years, every financial institution will have teams of AI analysts working alongside their sharpest minds. At Endex, we're on a mission to bridge the present to the inevitable by building the most sophisticated digital coworker native in Excel. We're a team working on the frontier of agentic development and applied finance, in close collaboration with leading research labs and backed by OpenAI ($14M Raise in Aug. 2025).

With significant demand after our launch, financial firms across the country recognize agentic AI in financial modeling is the future.



About the Role

Location: New York City Commitment: 5-6 Days In-Person

Brand marketers at Endex shape how the most demanding buyers in finance encounter our work. You'll generate the ideas and ship them end-to-end: brief, vendors, production, launch, and measurement. If you thrive in ambiguity and want to build the brand of a category-defining company, let's chat.



Your Key Responsibilities

 - Own the Brand Voice: Shape Endex's brand across every public surface including website, newsletter, LinkedIn, launch announcements, and sales collateral.

 - Run Campaigns End-to-End: Lead the creative, production, and measurement of Endex's brand campaigns across OOH, conferences, social media, launches.

 - Manage Agencies and Vendors: Build a creative bench across design, video, and production as we scale.

 - Partner Across the Company: Work with product, sales, and leadership to translate complex capabilities into language finance professionals find credible and compelling.

 - Measure and Iterate: Bring analytical rigor to every campaign to know what worked, what didn't, and what to run next.
   
   

What We're Looking For

 - Creative and Analytical: You generate the idea, write the brief, hire the vendors, manage the budget, and ship the work.

 - Writer First: You write with the precision a finance audience demands, and your portfolio includes long-form work you personally produced and shipped.

 - Builder: You'd rather ship three good things this month than plan one perfect thing for next quarter.

 - Taste: You have a campaign from any company, any category that you genuinely admire, and you can articulate exactly why it works.

 - Extensible: You adapt fast as the product and company evolve around you.

 - 3+ years of experience in brand marketing



Full-Time Benefits

 - Health & Dental Insurance

 - Monthly Wellness Stipend

 - Retirement Benefits - 401K Matching

 - Competitive Salary + Equity

source_jobs.title: Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are

Over the next few years, every financial institution will have teams of AI analysts working alongside their sharpest minds. At Endex, we're on a mission to bridge the present to the inevitable by building the most sophisticated digital coworker native in Excel. We're a team working on the frontier of agentic development and applied finance, in close collaboration with leading research labs and backed by OpenAI ($14M Raise in Aug. 2025).

With significant demand after our launch, financial firms across the country recognize that agentic AI in financial modeling is the future. Now we're building the team that will take us there.



About the Role

Location: New York City

At Endex, you'll own the full talent lifecycle, from identifying the best minds at universities and institutions to closing offers on candidates who are genuinely excited to build something transformational. If you're energized by finding exceptional people, telling a compelling story, and building something from scratch, this role is for you.



Your Key Responsibilities

 - Own Full-Cycle Recruiting: Source, screen, interview, and close candidates in engineering

 - Build Pipelines: Develop and maintain relationships with top universities and programs, establishing Endex as a destination for exceptional graduating talent

 - Sell the Endex Story: Represent Endex authentically and compellingly to candidates. You'll be a brand ambassador who can speak fluently about our product, mission, and the opportunity to join at a pivotal moment.

 - Design the Hiring Infrastructure: Build and refine the systems, scorecards, take-home assignments, and processes that make Endex's hiring consistent, fast, and high-signal.

 - Partner with Leadership: Work directly with the CEO and hiring managers to understand team needs, define role requirements, and move quickly when the right candidate is in the pipeline.

 - Analyze and Improve: Track recruiting metrics, identify bottlenecks, and continuously sharpen the process to reduce time-to-hire without sacrificing quality.



What We're Looking For

 - Talent Spotter: You have strong pattern recognition for exceptional people. You know the difference between a polished resume and a genuine standout, and you know how to find the latter.

 - Compelling Storyteller: You can speak to candidates about why Endex is the most exciting place to build right now.

 - Relentlessly Resourceful: You can pipelines, tap your network, show up on campuses, and work hard to find the best talent pools

 - Process Builder: You think systematically about how great hiring works and you build the infrastructure to make it repeatable.

 - Execution-Oriented: You manage multiple roles and candidates simultaneously without things falling through the cracks. Candidates hear back from you, processes move forward, and offers go out on time.



Nice to Haves

 - Experience recruiting at an early-stage startup

 - Background in or strong familiarity with finance, fintech, or AI

 - Existing relationships with recruiting programs and talent networks

 - Experience building recruiting infrastructure from scratch



Full-Time Benefits

 - Health & Dental Insurance

 - Monthly Wellness Stipend

 - Retirement Benefits — 401K Matching

 - Competitive Salary + Equity

notes: Endex is a startup working on agentic development and applied finance, with a strong focus on engineering and AI. The company has a small but growing team and is backed by OpenAI.
